source_name: FIPS 140-3 — Security Requirements for Cryptographic Modules
requirement: Establish documented security policies defining roles, services, and authentication for cryptographic modules as required by the standard.
evidence_quote: The security requirements cover areas related to the secure design, implementation and operation of a cryptographic module. These areas include ... roles, services, and authentication...

source_name: FIPS 140-3 — Security Requirements for Cryptographic Modules
requirement: Ensure the responsible authority in each agency verifies that the security provided by a module is sufficient and acceptable to the information owner.
evidence_quote: The responsible authority in each agency shall ensure that the security of the system is sufficient and acceptable.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 — Security Requirements for Cryptographic Modules
requirement: Acknowledge and accept any residual risk associated with the cryptographic module's security posture.
evidence_quote: The operator of a cryptographic module is responsible for ensuring that the security provided by a module is sufficient and acceptable to the owner of the information that is being protected and that any residual risk is acknowledged and accepted.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 — Security Requirements for Cryptographic Modules
requirement: Utilize independent, accredited Cryptographic and Security Testing (CST) laboratories to validate cryptographic modules against FIPS 140-3.
evidence_quote: In the CMVP, vendors of cryptographic modules use independent, accredited Cryptographic and Security Testing (CST) laboratories to have their modules tested.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 — Security Requirements for Cryptographic Modules
requirement: Employ only approved security functions, including algorithms and key management techniques, specified in FIPS or referenced NIST Special Publications.
evidence_quote: Cryptographic modules that conform to this standard shall employ Approved security functions such as cryptographic algorithms, cryptographic key management techniques, and authentication techniques that have been approved for protecting Federal government sensitive information.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 — Security Requirements for Cryptographic Modules
requirement: Submit test reports demonstrating compliance to the CMVP for review and validation prior to deployment.
evidence_quote: A test report for modules demonstrating compliance will be submitted to the CMVP for review and validation.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 — Security Requirements for Cryptographic Modules
requirement: Develop acquisition plans for FIPS 140-3 compliant products and avoid using the CMVP Historical list for procurement decisions.
evidence_quote: Agencies should develop plans for the acquisition of products that are compliant with FIPS 140-3; however, agencies may purchase any of the products on the CMVP validated modules list. (Note: the CMVP Historical list should not be used for procurement decisions.)

source_name: FIPS 140-3 — Security Requirements for Cryptographic Modules
requirement: Review the standard at least every five years to consider necessary updates or replacement due to technological changes.
evidence_quote: This standard will be reviewed at least every five years in order to consider necessary updates or replacement.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography
requirement: Identify embedded or bound validated modules by name, certificate number, and version in the Security Policy and test report.
evidence_quote: The FIPS 140-3 IUT submission (e.g., Security Policy and validation test report) shall precisely identify the EVM by name, CMVP certificate number and version(s).

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography
requirement: Ensure the existing validated module (EVM) status is Active at the time of submission to the CMVP.
evidence_quote: The EVM status shall be Active (i.e., is not on the Historical or Revoked list) at the time of the IUT submission to the CMVP.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography
requirement: Verify that the operational environment of the embedded algorithm implementation matches the module's tested environment.
evidence_quote: The operational environment under which the validated cryptographic algorithm implementation was tested by the CAVP must be identical to, or fully included in the operational environment that the cryptographic module is being tested under.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography
requirement: Demonstrate how the IUT meets FIPS 140-3 requirements when relying on an EVM for specific functions.
evidence_quote: If the IUT relies on the EVM to meet one or more FIPS 140-3 requirement... the IUT submission shall demonstrate in the Test Report how the relied upon FIPS requirement(s) is met without being non-compliant to any other FIPS 140-3 requirement.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography
requirement: Accept that the IUT inherits the Historical validation status if the EVM moves to the Historical list.
evidence_quote: The IUT shall inherit the Historical validation status if the EVM moves to the historical list for any reason (e.g., is sunset or an algorithm transition).

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography
requirement: Re-test algorithm implementations when porting to a different processor bit size or operating system.
evidence_quote: If an implementation has been tested on an X-bit processor... a claim cannot be made that the implementation also runs on different bit size processors... the algorithm implementations must be re-tested.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography
requirement: Mark all references to EVM functionality with [EVM] in the Security Policy tables to distinguish IUT and EVM services.
evidence_quote: This shall be done per below by marking '[EVM]' to the start of each applicable reference within the following WebCyptik tables... All other areas of the Security Policy where EVM information is needed... shall also be marked with [EVM].

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography
requirement: Ensure the EVM security level is equal to or higher than the IUT for bound modules, except for Mitigation of Other Attacks.
evidence_quote: For bound modules, the EVM shall be the same or higher security level as the IUT for all sections of the FIPS 140-3 standard, except for Mitigation of other attacks which can differ in security levels.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography
requirement: Prohibit binding or embedding a FIPS 140-3 IUT to a FIPS 140-2 EVM.
evidence_quote: A FIPS 140-3 IUT cannot embed or bind to a FIPS 140-2 EVM.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance for Post-Quantum Cryptography
requirement: Ensure new validation submissions obtaining entropy from a previously validated embedded module comply with SP 800-90B.
evidence_quote: Any new validation submission of a cryptographic module that obtains its entropy from a previously-validated embedded module shall comply with SP 800-90B.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance — September 2025 PQC update
requirement: Identify embedded or bound validated modules by name, certificate number, and version in the Security Policy and test report.
evidence_quote: The FIPS 140-3 IUT submission (e.g., Security Policy and validation test report) shall precisely identify the EVM by name, CMVP certificate number and version(s).

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance — September 2025 PQC update
requirement: Mark all references to EVM functionality with [EVM] in Security Policy tables to distinguish IUT from EVM capabilities.
evidence_quote: This shall be done per below by marking '[EVM]' to the start of each applicable reference within the following WebCyptik tables

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance — September 2025 PQC update
requirement: Ensure the Existing Validated Module (EVM) status is Active at the time of IUT submission to the CMVP.
evidence_quote: The EVM status shall be Active (i.e., is not on the Historical or Revoked list) at the time of the IUT submission to the CMVP.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance — September 2025 PQC update
requirement: Verify that the operational environment of the validated algorithm implementation matches the module's tested environment exactly.
evidence_quote: The operational environment under which the validated cryptographic algorithm implementation was tested by the CAVP must be identical to, or fully included in the operational environment that the cryptographic module is being tested under

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance — September 2025 PQC update
requirement: Demonstrate in the Test Report how the IUT meets FIPS 140-3 requirements when relying on an EVM for specific functions.
evidence_quote: If the IUT relies on the EVM to meet one or more FIPS 140-3 requirement... then the IUT submission shall demonstrate in the Test Report how the relied upon FIPS requirement(s) is met

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance — September 2025 PQC update
requirement: Accept that the IUT inherits the Historical validation status if the EVM moves to the Historical list.
evidence_quote: The IUT shall inherit the Historical validation status if the EVM moves to the historical list for any reason (e.g., is sunset or an algorithm transition).

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance — September 2025 PQC update
requirement: Re-validate algorithm implementations when porting to new hardware or different processor bit sizes.
evidence_quote: If an algorithm implementation has been tested on an X-bit processor... a claim cannot be made that the implementation also runs on different bit size processors... the algorithm implementations must be re-tested

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance — September 2025 PQC update
requirement: Re-validate algorithm implementations when moving from one operating system to another.
evidence_quote: If an algorithm implementation has been tested on one operating system, a claim cannot be made that the implementation also runs on another operating system when it is considered for module testing.

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance — September 2025 PQC update
requirement: Provide a mapping of HMI ports to physical I/O pins or internal test interfaces to ensure provable control during testing.
evidence_quote: For operational testing purposes, access to the HMI ports shall be available and a mapping shall be provided. These may be mapped to physical I/O pins, internal test interfaces

source_name: FIPS 140-3 Implementation Guidance — September 2025 PQC update
requirement: Verify and document vendor rationale for risks posed by intervening functional subsystems in sub-chip cryptographic subsystems.
evidence_quote: The tester shall verify and provide the vendor's rationale in the validation report (TE02.13.01) explaining existing risks and mitigations.

source_name: CMVP Management Manual
requirement: Define and document roles and responsibilities for vendors, laboratories, and validation authorities within the CMVP framework.
evidence_quote: This manual outlines the management activities and specific responsibilities which have been assigned to the various participating groups.

source_name: CMVP Management Manual
requirement: Establish formal channels for requesting official guidance on FIPS 140-2 test requirements and policy interpretations.
evidence_quote: If an official response is requested, then an official request must be submitted to the CMVP written in the Request for Guidance (RFG) format described below.

source_name: CMVP Management Manual
requirement: Maintain confidentiality agreements and non-disclosure protocols for proprietary information exchanged between NIST, CCCS, and CST laboratories.
evidence_quote: Confidentiality of Proprietary Information Exchanged between NIST, CCCS and the CST Laboratory... Non-Disclosure Agreement for Current and Former Employees.

source_name: CMVP Management Manual
requirement: Engage only NVLAP accredited Cryptographic and Security Testing (CST) laboratories to perform conformance testing for module validation.
evidence_quote: Vendors of commercial cryptographic modules use independent, National Voluntary Laboratory Accreditation Program (NVLAP) accredited Cryptographic and Security Testing (CST) laboratories to have their modules tested.

source_name: CMVP Management Manual
requirement: Submit validation reports and evidence for review and approval by the CMVP Validation Authorities to obtain a validation certificate.
evidence_quote: NIST and CCCS, as the joint CMVP Validation Authorities, review laboratory reports, issue validation certificates, and participate in laboratory accreditations.

source_name: CMVP Management Manual
requirement: Monitor the official CMVP website for updates to validation lists, notices, and programmatic policy changes.
evidence_quote: The CMVP website contains several pages pertinent to the program: Announcements... Notices... Validation Lists... Modules in Process.

source_name: CMVP Management Manual
requirement: Collect and report programmatic metrics regarding validation activities using the METRIX Collection Tool.
evidence_quote: Section 5 – CMVP and CAVP Programmatic Metrics Collection provides an overview of the CMVP and CAVP Programmatic Metrics Collection and a description of the collection and reporting processes of the CMVP metrics.

source_name: CMVP Management Manual
requirement: Adhere to validation deadlines and manage the status of modules in process, including handling HOLD statuses.
evidence_quote: 4.4.3 HOLD Status for Cryptographic Modules on the Modules In Process List... 4.4.4 Validation Deadline

source_name: CMVP Management Manual
requirement: Follow established processes for validation revocation and flaw discovery handling to maintain module integrity.
evidence_quote: 4.8 Flaw Discovery Handling Process... 4.9 Validation Revocation

source_name: Network and Information Security Directive 2 (NIS2)
requirement: Implement risk-management measures including policy, incident handling, and supply chain security for network and information systems.
evidence_quote: Essential and important entities shall take appropriate and proportionate technical, operational and organisational measures to manage the risks posed to the security of their network and information systems.

source_name: Network and Information Security Directive 2 (NIS2)
requirement: Establish and enforce policies for asset inventory, vulnerability management, and business continuity planning.
evidence_quote: Measures shall include, inter alia: (a) risk analysis and management; (b) incident handling; (c) business continuity management; (d) supply chain security; (e) security of network and information systems development and deployment.

source_name: Network and Information Security Directive 2 (NIS2)
requirement: Ensure senior management is accountable for cybersecurity risk management and approves relevant policies.
evidence_quote: Member States shall ensure that the management bodies of essential and important entities are involved in the decision-making process regarding cybersecurity risk management measures and are accountable for the implementation of those measures.

source_name: Network and Information Security Directive 2 (NIS2)
requirement: Maintain records of significant incidents and submit notifications to competent authorities within strict timeframes.
evidence_quote: Essential and important entities shall notify competent authorities of significant incidents without undue delay and no later than 24 hours after becoming aware of the incident.

source_name: Network and Information Security Directive 2 (NIS2)
requirement: Provide evidence of compliance with cybersecurity risk-management measures upon request by supervisory authorities.
evidence_quote: Competent authorities shall have the power to require essential and important entities to provide information and evidence regarding the implementation of cybersecurity risk-management measures.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140A — CMVP Documentation Requirements
requirement: Provide vendor documentation meeting minimum validation authority requirements for cryptographic modules undergoing independent verification.
evidence_quote: The vendor shall provide documentation for a cryptographic module that fulfils but is not limited to the minimum documentation requirements as specified in the validation authority requirement and management documentation.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140A — CMVP Documentation Requirements
requirement: Verify that vendors supply documentation fulfilling minimum validation authority requirements for cryptographic modules.
evidence_quote: The tester shall verify that the vendor provides documentation for a cryptographic module that fulfils but is not limited to the minimum documentation requirements as specified in the validation authority requirement and management documentation.

source_name: Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA) — Regulation EU 2022/2554
requirement: Establish documented ICT risk management policies and specific mechanisms for handling and reporting major ICT-related incidents.
evidence_quote: financial entities need to have comprehensive capabilities to enable a strong and effective ICT risk management, as well as specific mechanisms and policies for handling all ICT-related incidents and for reporting major ICT-related incidents.

source_name: Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA) — Regulation EU 2022/2554
requirement: Implement policies for testing ICT systems, controls, and processes to ensure digital operational resilience.
evidence_quote: financial entities should have policies in place for the testing of ICT systems, controls and processes, as well as for managing ICT third-party risk.

source_name: Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA) — Regulation EU 2022/2554
requirement: Define key principles and core contractual rights to manage ICT third-party risk and monitor subcontracting processes.
evidence_quote: it is necessary to set out certain key principles to guide financial entities' management of ICT third-party risk... accompanied by a set of core contractual rights... to strengthen financial entities' ability to effectively monitor all ICT risk.

source_name: Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA) — Regulation EU 2022/2554
requirement: Establish dedicated management functions and internal risk management models for non-microenterprises to supervise ICT third-party arrangements.
evidence_quote: only financial entities that are not microenterprises... should be required to establish more complex governance arrangements... set up dedicated management functions... or to set up an internal risk management and control model.

source_name: Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA) — Regulation EU 2022/2554
requirement: Submit the ICT risk management framework to internal audits to verify compliance and effectiveness.
evidence_quote: Such entities are better equipped... to submit their ICT risk management framework to internal audits.

source_name: Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA) — Regulation EU 2022/2554
requirement: Maintain a robust ICT-related incident reporting regime to provide competent authorities with a complete overview of incident nature and impact.
evidence_quote: this Regulation should lay down a robust ICT-related incident reporting regime whereby the relevant requirements address current gaps... It is essential to harmonise the ICT-related incident reporting regime.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140B: CMVP Security Policy Requirements
requirement: Establish documented security policy content and ordering for cryptographic modules as mandated by the validation authority.
evidence_quote: SP 800-140B also specifies the content of the tabular and graphical information required in ISO/IEC 19790 Annex B. As a validation authority, the Cryptographic Module Validation Program (CMVP) may... specify the order of the security policy as specified in ISO/IEC 19790:2012 B.1.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140B: CMVP Security Policy Requirements
requirement: Modify, add, or delete Vendor Evidence and Test Evidence to align with validation authority requirements for cryptographic modules.
evidence_quote: As a validation authority, the Cryptographic Module Validation Program (CMVP) may modify, add, or delete Vendor Evidence (VE) and/or Test Evidence (TE) specified under paragraph 6.14 of the ISO/IEC 24759

source_name: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) — Regulation EU 2016/679
requirement: Establish documented policies defining lawful bases, purposes, and retention limits for personal data processing.
evidence_quote: The specific purposes for which personal data are processed should be explicit and legitimate and determined at the time of the collection of the personal data. ... time limits should be established by the controller for erasure or for a periodic review.

source_name: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) — Regulation EU 2016/679
requirement: Implement technical and organizational measures to ensure pseudonymization and separate storage of attribution data.
evidence_quote: measures of pseudonymisation should, whilst allowing general analysis, be possible within the same controller when that controller has taken technical and organisational measures necessary to ensure, for the processing concerned, that this Regulation is implemented, and that additional information for attributing the personal data to a specific data subject is kept separately.

source_name: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) — Regulation EU 2016/679
requirement: Define and document the identity of the controller and the purposes of processing to ensure transparency.
evidence_quote: That principle concerns, in particular, information to the data subjects on the identity of the controller and the purposes of the processing and further information to ensure fair and transparent processing in respect of the natural persons concerned

source_name: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) — Regulation EU 2016/679
requirement: Maintain records demonstrating that data subjects have given valid consent for processing operations.
evidence_quote: Where processing is based on the data subject's consent, the controller should be able to demonstrate that the data subject has given consent to the processing operation.

source_name: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) — Regulation EU 2016/679
requirement: Ensure processing activities comply with applicable data protection rules and are subject to monitoring by supervisory authorities.
evidence_quote: The processing of personal data by those public authorities should comply with the applicable data-protection rules according to the purposes of the processing.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Designate a supervisory body to carry out supervisory activities under the Regulation.
evidence_quote: Member States should designate a supervisory body or supervisory bodies to carry out the supervisory activities under this Regulation.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Apply good security practice appropriate to the risks related to trust service activities.
evidence_quote: It should be incumbent on all trust service providers to apply good security practice appropriate to the risks related to their activities so as to boost users' trust in the single market.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Ensure due diligence, transparency, and accountability of operations and services.
evidence_quote: All trust service providers should be subject to the requirements of this Regulation, in particular those on security and liability to ensure due diligence, transparency and accountability of their operations and services.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Establish liability for damage caused by failure to comply with regulatory obligations.
evidence_quote: This Regulation should provide for the liability of all trust service providers. In particular, it establishes the liability regime under which all trust service providers should be liable for damage caused to any natural or legal person due to failure to comply with the obligations under this Regulation.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Implement termination plans to ensure sustainability and durability of qualified trust services.
evidence_quote: To ensure sustainability and durability of qualified trust services and to boost users' confidence in the continuity of qualified trust services, supervisory bodies should verify the existence and the correct application of provisions on termination plans in cases where qualified trust service providers cease their activities.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Submit conformity assessment reports to the supervisory body to ensure compliance.
evidence_quote: In order to ensure the compliance of qualified trust service providers and the services they provide with the requirements set out in this Regulation, a conformity assessment should be carried out by a conformity assessment body and the resulting conformity assessment reports should be submitted by the qualified trust service providers to the supervisory body.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Report security incidents and personal data breaches to supervisory bodies and data protection authorities.
evidence_quote: Supervisory bodies should cooperate with data protection authorities, for example, by informing them about the results of audits of qualified trust service providers, where personal data protection rules appear to have been breached. The provision of information should in particular cover security incidents and personal data breaches.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Notify security breaches and conduct security risk assessments to provide adequate information.
evidence_quote: Notification of security breaches and security risk assessments is essential with a view to providing adequate information to concerned parties in the event of a breach of security or loss of integrity.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Report supervisory activities to the Commission and ENISA to assess effectiveness.
evidence_quote: To enable the Commission and the Member States to assess the effectiveness of the enhanced supervision mechanism introduced by this Regulation, supervisory bodies should be requested to report on their activities.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Ensure long-term preservation of information to guarantee legal validity over extended periods.
evidence_quote: This Regulation should ensure the long-term preservation of information, in order to ensure the legal validity of electronic signatures and electronic seals over extended periods of time and guarantee that they can be validated irrespective of future technological changes.

source_name: eIDAS Regulation — Regulation EU 910/2014 (eIDAS 1.0)
requirement: Clearly indicate the suspension status and precise period of time for suspended certificates.
evidence_quote: Legal certainty calls for the suspension status of a certificate to always be clearly indicated. To that end, trust service providers should have the responsibility to clearly indicate the status of the certificate and, if suspended, the precise period of time during which the certificate has been suspended.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Utilize only CMVP-approved methods for sensitive security parameter generation and establishment, precluding all other methods.
evidence_quote: This document identifies CMVP-approved sensitive security parameter generation and establishment methods. It precludes the use of all other sensitive security parameter generation and establishment methods.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Adhere to NIST SP 800-131A Rev. 2 for transitioning cryptographic algorithms and key lengths in parameter generation.
evidence_quote: Barker EB, Roginsky AL (2019) Transitioning the Use of Cryptographic Algorithms and Key Lengths. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-131A, Rev. 2.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Implement key establishment techniques for DSA, RSA, and ECDSA strictly according to FIPS 186-4.
evidence_quote: National Institute of Standards and Technology (2013) Digital Signature Standard (DSS). (U.S. Department of Commerce, Washington, DC), Federal Information Processing Standards Publication (FIPS) 186-4. DSA, RSA, and ECDSA.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Execute pair-wise key establishment using discrete logarithm cryptography per NIST SP 800-56A Rev. 3.
evidence_quote: Barker EB, Chen L, Roginsky AL, Vassilev A, Davis R (2018) Recommendation for Pair-Wise Key-Establishment Schemes Using Discrete Logarithm Cryptography. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-56A, Rev. 3.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Execute pair-wise key establishment using integer factorization cryptography per NIST SP 800-56B Rev. 2.
evidence_quote: Barker EB, Chen L, Roginsky AL, Vassilev A, Davis R, Simon S (2019) Recommendation for Pair-Wise Key-Establishment Using Integer Factorization Cryptography. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-56B, Rev. 2.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Derive keys using pseudorandom functions in accordance with NIST SP 800-108 Revised.
evidence_quote: Chen L (2009) Recommendation for Key Derivation Using Pseudorandom Functions (Revised). (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-108, Revised.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Derive keys from passwords for storage applications following NIST SP 800-132.
evidence_quote: Sönmez Turan M, Barker EB, Burr WE, Chen L (2010) Recommendation for Password-Based Key Derivation: Part 1: Storage Applications. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-132.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Utilize existing application-specific key derivation functions as defined in NIST SP 800-135 Rev. 1.
evidence_quote: Dang QH (2011) Recommendation for Existing Application-Specific Key Derivation Functions. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-135, Rev. 1.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Apply key-derivation methods in key-establishment schemes per NIST SP 800-56C Rev. 1.
evidence_quote: Barker EB, Chen L, Davis R (2018) Recommendation for Key-Derivation Methods in Key-Establishment Schemes. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-56C, Rev. 1.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Perform key derivation through extraction-then-expansion according to NIST SP 800-56C.
evidence_quote: Chen L (2011) Recommendation for Key-Derivation through Extraction-then-Expansion. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-56C.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Wrap keys using block cipher modes of operation as specified in NIST SP 800-38F.
evidence_quote: Dworkin MJ (2012) Recommendation for Block Cipher Modes of Operation: Methods for Key Wrapping. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-38F.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Generate cryptographic keys strictly following NIST SP 800-133 Rev. 1.
evidence_quote: Barker EB, Roginsky AL (2019) Recommendation for Cryptographic Key Generation. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-133, Rev. 1.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Implement deterministic random bit generators for random number generation per NIST SP 800-90A Rev. 1.
evidence_quote: Barker EB, Kelsey J (2015) Recommendation for Random Number Generation Using Deterministic Random Bit Generators. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-90A, Rev. 1.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140D — CMVP Approved SSP Generation and Establishment Methods
requirement: Validate entropy sources used for random number generation in accordance with NIST SP 800-90B.
evidence_quote: Sonmez Turan M, Barker EB, Kelsey J, McKay KA, Baish, ML, Boyle M (2018) Recommendation for Entropy Sources Used for Random Number Generation. (National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, MD), NIST Special Publication (SP) 800-90B.

source_name: EU Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) — Regulation EU 2024/2847
requirement: Establish documented policies and processes for cybersecurity risk management throughout the product lifecycle.
evidence_quote: manufacturers take security seriously throughout a product’s lifecycle... Manufacturers should therefore ensure that all products with digital elements are designed and developed in accordance with the essential cybersecurity requirements laid down in this Regulation.

source_name: EU Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) — Regulation EU 2024/2847
requirement: Ensure staff possesses necessary cybersecurity skills to comply with regulatory obligations.
evidence_quote: manufacturers should ensure that their staff has the necessary skills to comply with their obligations as laid down in this Regulation.

source_name: EU Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) — Regulation EU 2024/2847
requirement: Generate and maintain Software Bills of Materials (SBOMs) to document software components and dependencies.
evidence_quote: Market surveillance authorities should be able to request manufacturers of categories of products with digital elements established by ADCO to submit the software bills of materials (SBOMs) that they have generated pursuant to this Regulation.

source_name: EU Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) — Regulation EU 2024/2847
requirement: Implement processes to provide security updates and manage vulnerabilities throughout the product support period.
evidence_quote: insufficient and inconsistent provision of security updates to address them... ensuring that hardware and software products are placed on the market with fewer vulnerabilities and that manufacturers take security seriously throughout a product’s lifecycle.

source_name: EU Cyber Resilience Act (CRA) — Regulation EU 2024/2847
requirement: Undergo conformity assessment procedures to verify compliance with essential cybersecurity requirements before market placement.
evidence_quote: Consumer products with digital elements categorised in this Regulation as important products with digital elements... should undergo a stricter conformity assessment procedure.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140E — CMVP Approved Authentication Mechanisms
requirement: Ensure authentication mechanisms for cryptographic modules meet CMVP-approved requirements and align with SP 800-63B standards.
evidence_quote: This document includes all requirements for CMVP-approved authentication mechanisms for operators. Vendors should use SP 800-63B as a framework for authentication requirements and should provide justification whenever SP 800-63B requirements cannot be met.

source_name: NIST SP 800-140E — CMVP Approved Authentication Mechanisms
requirement: Validate that operator authentication is performed by the module or Operating Environment for FIPS 140-3 levels above Level 1.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Include the quantum threat in risk analysis and consider quantum protection measures for relevant cryptographic products.
evidence_quote: L'ANSSI encourage toutes les industries à inclure la menace quantique dans leur analyse de risque et à envisager d'inclure des mesures de protection quantique dans les produits cryptographiques concernés.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Mandate hybridation for products requiring post-quantum protection, except for specific hash-based signatures.
evidence_quote: L'ANSSI insiste toujours fortement sur la nécessité de l'hybridation partout où une protection post-quantique est nécessaire, à la fois à court et à moyen terme.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Dimension symmetric primitives to ensure post-quantum security, targeting at least AES-256 and SHA2-384 levels.
evidence_quote: l'ANSSI encourage également à dimensionner les paramètres des primitives symétriques de manière à assurer une sécurité post-quantique conjecturée ; en pratique, au moins le même niveau de sécurité que l'AES-256 pour les algorithmes de chiffrement par bloc et au moins le même niveau de sécurité que SHA2-384 pour les fonctions de hachage.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Avoid modifying normalized parameters for post-quantum algorithms like CRYSTALS-Kyber, Dilithium, Falcon, XMSS, and SPHINCS+.
evidence_quote: Il est important d'éviter de modifier les paramètres de l'instance normalisée.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Use the highest possible NIST security level, preferably Level 5 or Level 3, for post-quantum algorithms.
evidence_quote: Nous recommandons d'utiliser le niveau de sécurité NIST le plus élevé possible, de préférence le niveau 5 (équivalent à l'AES-256) ou le niveau 3 (équivalent à l'AES-192).

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Protect the internal state of stateful signature algorithms like XMSS and LMS against integrity attacks and replay attacks.
evidence_quote: L'état interne de l'algorithme de signature est une donnée très critique et doit être protégé en intégrité. Il doit également être protégé contre les attaques par rejeu.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Ensure pre-shared keys used in hybridation are shared only between two parties and maintain their confidentiality and integrity.
evidence_quote: La confidentialité et l'intégrité de la clé pré-partagée sont des conditions préalables essentielles. Chaque clé pré-partagée ne doit être partagée que par deux parties et non par un groupe de trois parties ou plus.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Use well-studied standards or modes with validated security proofs for cryptographic functions and hybridation.
evidence_quote: l'ANSSI recommande d'utiliser des normes ou des modes bien étudiés avec des preuves de sécurité validées.

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Require hybridation implementation for final products with post-quantum protection, unless using specific hash-based signatures.
evidence_quote: tout produit qui inclut une protection post-quantique doit mettre en œuvre l'hybridation, sauf dans les rares cas où la protection post-quantique repose uniquement sur des signatures basées sur le hachage comme XMSS, LMS ou SPHINCS+, pour lesquelles l'hybridation est facultative

source_name: ANSSI Views on Post-Quantum Cryptography Transition
requirement: Include user guides recommending exclusive use of post-quantum algorithms in combination with classical algorithms for platform products.
evidence_quote: l'inclusion dans les guides pour les utilisateurs d'une recommandation d'utiliser exclusivement l'algorithme post-quantique fourni en combinaison avec un algorithme classique reconnu dans le cadre d'un mode d'hybridation.

evidence_quote: Renegotiation initiated by the client should by rejected by the server.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Avoid using 0-RTT data in TLS 1.3 PSK handshakes due to lack of replay protection.
evidence_quote: Therefore, sending or accepting 0-RTT data is not recommended.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Ensure ephemeral keys are deleted irrevocably after use and never stored persistently.
evidence_quote: It must be ensured that all ephemeral keys are deleted irrevocably after they have been used and that no copies of these keys were made.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Store private cryptographic keys in certified hardware such as HSMs or chip cards.
evidence_quote: Secure storage of the keys can be achieved, for example by using certified hardware (chip card, HSM).

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Validate random number generators against BSI AIS 20/31 classes DRG.3, DRG.4, DRT.1, PTG.3, or NTG.1.
evidence_quote: A random number generator from one of the classes DRG.3, DRG.4, DRT.1, PTG.3 or NTG.1 according to [AIS 20/31] is recommended.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Inventory and restrict TLS cipher suites to those listed in the BSI recommendations with Perfect Forward Secrecy.
evidence_quote: The use of the following cipher suites with Perfect Forward Secrecy is recommended.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Inventory and restrict Diffie-Hellman groups to approved named curves to avoid weak domain parameters.
evidence_quote: It is recommended to only use named curves... in order to avoid attacks via unverified weak domain parameters.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for TLS
requirement: Plan migration to quantum-safe hybrid key agreement mechanisms by the end of 2031.
evidence_quote: The sole use of classic key agreement mechanisms is only recommended until the end of 2031.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Define security policies specifying SA lifetimes and data volume limits for renegotiation.
evidence_quote: Indicating binding periods of validity or an upper limit for the data volume is part of a security policy and must be defined by the system administrator.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Define security requirements for SA lifetimes based on application needs.
evidence_quote: The SA lifetime should be defined depending on the security requirements of the application.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Renegotiate IKE-SAs and IPsec-SAs upon expiration of defined time or data volume limits.
evidence_quote: Both an IKE-SA and an IPsec-SA should only be valid for a limited period of time and renegotiated after this period of time has expired.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Limit IKE-SA lifetime to 24 hours and IPsec-SA lifetime to 4 hours in standard scenarios.
evidence_quote: In ordinary operating scenarios, the IKE-SA lifetime should not exceed 24 h and the IPsec-SA lifetime should not exceed 4 h.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Irrevocably delete ephemeral keys immediately after use and prevent persistent storage.
evidence_quote: It must be ensured that all ephemeral keys are deleted irrevocably after they have been used and that no copies of these keys were made.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Delete nonces and long-term keys immediately after single use in signature generation.
evidence_quote: The nonce as well as the long-term key must be kept secret and deleted immediately after they have been used once.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Use random number generators from approved classes DRG.3, DRG.4, DRT.1, PTG.3, or NTG.1.
evidence_quote: A random number generator from one of the classes DRG.3, DRG.4, DRT.1, PTG.3 or NTG.1 according to [AIS 20/31] is recommended.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Store private cryptographic keys in certified hardware such as chip cards or HSMs.
evidence_quote: Secure storage of the keys can be achieved, for example by using certified hardware (chip card, HSM).

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Protect Security Association Database and Security Policy Database from manipulation.
evidence_quote: The SAD and SPD databases must be stored in a secure manner in order to prevent them from being manipulated by attackers.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Identify and mitigate side-channel and fault attack vulnerabilities through expert involvement.
evidence_quote: The developer should identify possible side channels by involving experts in this field and implement corresponding countermeasures.

source_name: Cryptographic Mechanisms: Recommendations for IPsec
requirement: Migrate from sole classical key agreement mechanisms to hybrid quantum-safe mechanisms by end of 2031.
